#NMDPPartner, this campaign was never about one story. It’s about thousands. Like Cayden, who’s been fighting leukemia since he was 3. Like Noah, who joined the registry and saved a stranger’s life. Like Emilee and Riley – both post-transplant and living leukemia-free. Like the researchers, doctors, and families who show up every single day. They’re the reason this matters. Join the registry at nmdp.org/miquela. You could be someone’s match.

#NMDPPartner Thank you to my friend✨JoJo Siwa✨ for joining the cause!!! A true rockstar! 🤩 JoJo is right ya’ll—it is so easy to get on the NMDP Donor Registry and join the movement that helps patients like Cayden find their match. So sign up. Swab. Send your kit back. And when you get the call, say yes! You could change someone’s life forever!! 🔗 Link in bio.

#NMDPPartner I just talked to an NMDP patient navigator (new friend alert) who helps people through the cancer journey. I was lowkey nervous going into it, but honestly? Hearing how much support patients get and learning more about the NMDP Registry made me feel a little bit better. Join it at the link in my bio.

video_caption: Just because some people have it worse doesn’t mean my medical struggles are less important and that my emotions aren’t valid. Lyme disease is a horrible condition, it's not easy to treat (especially when gone untreated for far too long) and most people live with debilitating symptoms for the rest of their life. Of course I am thankful I don’t have something like cancer, but again, that doesn’t mean someone’s diagnosis of chronic illness should be downplayed. And yes believe it or not, I’ve received a PLETHORA of comments like this 🤦🏻‍♀️ #lymedisease #lymediseaseawareness #chroniclymedisease #chronicpain #chronicillness #chronicillnessawareness

video_caption: Will this be the era where we see the downfall of influencers??? 🤔🤔 I think if creators don’t begin to adjust the type of content they post, yes they will face the repercussions of that. There are creators who create content to actually connect with their audience and there are creators who create content with only one goal in mind — to get brand deals. As an influencer myself, I know that working with brands is how we make 98% of our income, but when every other video you post is an ad, people get turned off REAL quick. People also don’t want to constantly see you flaunt how much money you have when most people are struggling & working their butts off at their 9-5. Alix Earle & Emilie Kiser are the two influencers that come to mind who are extremely successful yet are very humble when it comes to their wealth. I think it’s awesome & inspiring to see successful creators on here, & seeing their videos on occasion of them treating themselves with a new designer bag or something is okay!!! Of course I continue to work with brands, this is my job, but I have become very picky because accepting every brand deal has already affected my growth and engagement in the past and I refuse to let that happen again. I also blame a lot of these brands for being so picky when it comes to the videos that influencers post. Every brand sends outs a brief and you have to follow the brief to a T! Very rarely will a brand allow a creator to have full control over the content which is sad. I think if more brands treated brand deals on TikTok & Instagram as they do on YouTube where the product is integrated into a video, the messaging would come off much more effectively & these brands would see larger conversion #influencers #influencermarketing #relatable #controversial #outoftouch
video_stickers:
WHY are influencers not
relatable anymore? 
(my pov as an influencer)
